content.title: Voice-profile routing architecture
content.summary: Following the Linear pull that extended docs/voice-profile.md (renamed and relocated to claude-code-shared/resources/voice-profiles/dev-office.md, wired directly into pr-revise), Eric wanted to explore a router rather than hand-wiring a profile path into each skill. A grill session worked through whether a router is warranted, what axis it should route on, what shape it should take, and how pr-code-review's existing hand-authored Tone section relates to the evidence-based dev-office voice profile. Landed on: skill-level routing, built now via a new voice-routing.json mirroring model-tiers.json's shape, one shared voice profile (dev-office) for both pr-code-review and pr-revise, and task-specific posture rules (pr-code-review's Tone section, pr-revise's reply quality bar) staying inline per-skill rather than extracted into shared infrastructure, since neither currently has a second consumer.
content.problem_statement: dev-office.md (Eric's evidence-based writing-voice profile) was wired into pr-revise via a single hardcoded file path added directly to its SKILL.md. This does not scale: Eric anticipates more than one voice profile (e.g. a future marketing profile) and more skills wanting voice guidance, and pr-code-review already has its own separate, hand-authored 'Tone' section that was never grounded in Eric's actual writing and risked being treated as a second, competing voice definition.
content.solution: Introduce a dedicated router config, claude-code-shared/resources/voice-routing.json, structured like model-tiers.json (a profiles map of name -> {file, description}, and a skills map of skill name -> profile name, with room for an array of profile names for future composability). Route on skill identity, not repo, since voice tracks the kind of writing being produced rather than where the work happens. Both pr-code-review and pr-revise route to the single dev-office profile. Extend register-skill's existing registration chain (the same chain that maintains model-tiers.json's skills/agents maps) to also assign/verify each newly registered skill's voice-routing.json entry, so future skills get wired automatically instead of via a one-off manual edit. Task-specific posture logic (how to behave given the voice, e.g. hedge-and-ask vs assert-directly, mandatory praise, confidence gating) is explicitly distinguished from voice (how words sound) and is left inline in each skill's own SKILL.md rather than extracted into shared posture infrastructure, because neither pr-code-review's nor pr-revise's posture rules currently have a second consumer to justify the extraction.
decisions: Voice-profile routing axis is skill-level, not repo-level: voice tracks what kind of writing is being produced, not which repo the work happens in. Eric confirmed he has other repos with different tones, but those are still driven by which skill/task is running, not by repo location.

Build the voice-routing router now rather than waiting for a second profile or skill to justify it, because register-skill already provides an enforcement mechanism (it keeps model-tiers.json's skill/agent maps in sync on registration) that a voice map can piggyback on for near-zero added cost, avoiding a bespoke manual SKILL.md edit for every future skill.

The router lives in its own file, claude-code-shared/resources/voice-routing.json, not as a new section inside model-tiers.json, matching the existing one-file-per-concern convention (task-routing.json, repo-policy.json, model-tiers.json each own a single concern) and keeping it independent of sync-model-tiers.py, which is scoped to model/effort tiers only.

voice-routing.json mirrors model-tiers.json's two-level shape: a profiles map (profile name -> file path + short description) and a skills map (skill name -> profile name, with a name-or-array shape to allow future composability without a schema change).

register-skill's registration chain is extended to also assign/verify a new skill's voice-routing.json entry, the same way it already handles model-tiers.json's skills/agents maps, so voice wiring happens automatically at registration time rather than as a separate manual step.

pr-code-review and pr-revise share a single voice profile (dev-office), rather than each getting its own voice file, because both are the same domain of writing (technical dev/office comments to colleagues) and the dev-office.md corpus already includes Eric's own inline PR review comments, not only his replies as a PR author, so there is no evidence gap that would justify a second voice profile.

pr-code-review's existing hand-authored 'Tone' section (ask-vs-assert when unsure, mandatory praise, confidence-gating strong labels) and pr-revise's 'Reply-body quality bar' section are posture, not voice: task-specific behavioral policy for what to do before speaking, as opposed to voice, which is how the words sound once you've decided to say them. This is the conceptual line that resolves whether they compete with dev-office.md: they don't, they operate on a different axis and layer on top of the same voice.

Posture stays inline in each skill's own SKILL.md for now, not extracted into a shared file or into voice-routing.json, because neither pr-code-review's posture nor pr-revise's posture currently has a second consumer; extraction should happen if/when a future skill genuinely wants to reuse one of them (e.g. a future 'review this PRD'-style skill reusing pr-code-review's critique posture).

Renaming pr-code-review's 'Tone' section header (e.g. to 'Review posture') is optional and purely cosmetic/documentation clarity; it has no functional effect on its own and is not required by any of the above decisions.

pr-revise's current voice wiring (a hardcoded reference to claude-code-shared/resources/voice-profiles/dev-office.md added directly in Step 8b during the Linear-pull session) is provisional and should be replaced with a lookup through voice-routing.json once that config exists, rather than remaining a hand-written path, so both pr-revise and pr-code-review go through the same mechanism.
implementation_decisions: voice-routing.json profiles map entries carry at least {file, description}; file is a path relative to claude-code-shared/resources/voice-profiles/.

voice-routing.json skills map entries accept either a single profile name (string) or an array of profile names, to support future blended/composable voice use without a later schema migration, even though no current skill needs more than one.

register-skill's registration chain gains a step that prompts for (or defaults) a voice profile assignment for the skill being registered, writing it into voice-routing.json's skills map, mirroring how it already writes into model-tiers.json's skills/agents maps.
risks_and_tradeoffs: If posture is extracted into shared infrastructure before a second real consumer exists, it repeats the exact anti-pattern this session avoided (reviewer-tone was invented, ungrounded content); the agreed trigger is a genuine second consumer, not anticipated reuse.

The skills-map name-or-array shape provisions for composability (a skill blending more than one voice profile) that is currently untested — no skill exercises it yet, so its exact blending semantics (how conflicts between two profiles resolve) are unproven until a real case arrives.
further_notes: dev-office.md itself (the voice profile content) was already extended with Linear-derived signal and relocated from docs/voice-profile.md to claude-code-shared/resources/voice-profiles/dev-office.md in the session immediately prior to this one; that work is not re-litigated here.

pr-code-review's Tone section content is unchanged by this seed's decisions; only its relationship to dev-office.md (not competing, operating on a different axis) and its optional header label are addressed.
content.next_action: Run /to-tasks to build voice-routing.json, wire pr-code-review and pr-revise to look up their voice profile from it (replacing pr-revise's hardcoded path), and extend register-skill's registration chain to assign each newly registered skill's voice-routing.json entry.

tasks.title: Create voice-routing.json router config
tasks.description: Add claude-code-shared/resources/voice-routing.json, a new router config mirroring model-tiers.json's two-level shape: a `profiles` map (profile name -> {file, description}) and a `skills` map (skill name -> profile name, using a string-or-array value to allow future multi-profile composability without a schema change). Seed the profiles map with a single `dev-office` entry pointing at resources/voice-profiles/dev-office.md, and seed the skills map with pr-code-review and pr-revise both routed to `dev-office`. This is a standalone config file, not a section inside model-tiers.json, independent of sync-model-tiers.py.
acceptance_criteria: voice-routing.json parses as valid JSON (python3 -m json.tool claude-code-shared/resources/voice-routing.json)
profiles.dev-office.file resolves to an existing file at claude-code-shared/resources/voice-profiles/dev-office.md
skills map contains entries for both pr-code-review and pr-revise, each valued 'dev-office'
tasks.summary: Created claude-code-shared/resources/voice-routing.json, a standalone router config mirroring model-tiers.json two-level shape, mapping pr-code-review and pr-revise to a seeded dev-office voice profile.

tasks.title: Wire pr-code-review to resolve its voice profile from voice-routing.json
tasks.description: Add a step to claude-code-shared/skills/pr-code-review/SKILL.md that looks up 'pr-code-review' in voice-routing.json's skills map, resolves the mapped profile name to its file path in the profiles map, and reads that voice-profile file (dev-office.md) before drafting review comments. The existing hand-authored Tone section stays as-is and is unaffected: it defines posture (ask-vs-assert, mandatory praise, confidence gating), a different axis from voice (how the words sound), so the new wiring adds voice grounding on top of, not instead of, Tone.
acceptance_criteria: pr-code-review/SKILL.md contains a step that reads the skill's profile from voice-routing.json's skills map and loads the resolved profile file, with no hardcoded path to dev-office.md
Manual check: running the lookup step for 'pr-code-review' resolves to claude-code-shared/resources/voice-profiles/dev-office.md
tasks.summary: Added a Step 1a to pr-code-review/SKILL.md that resolves the skill's voice profile at run time by looking up 'pr-code-review' in voice-routing.json's skills map and reading the mapped profile file from voice-profiles/, with no hardcoded path; added a one-sentence Tone-vs-voice clarification placed before the Tone heading so the Tone section body itself stays byte-identical.

tasks.title: Wire pr-revise to resolve its voice profile from voice-routing.json instead of a hardcoded path
tasks.description: Replace the hardcoded reference to claude-code-shared/resources/voice-profiles/dev-office.md at pr-revise/SKILL.md Step 8b (Reply-body quality bar) with a lookup through voice-routing.json's skills map, the same mechanism used by pr-code-review. Step 8b's downstream instructions (write reply_body in this voice, match reviewer register, ground claims in code) are unchanged; only the profile-resolution mechanism changes from a hand-written path to a router lookup.
acceptance_criteria: pr-revise/SKILL.md Step 8b no longer hardcodes the dev-office.md path; it looks up 'pr-revise' in voice-routing.json's skills map and resolves the profile file from there
Manual check: running the lookup step for 'pr-revise' resolves to claude-code-shared/resources/voice-profiles/dev-office.md
Reply-body quality bar's other instructions (register matching, grounding, first-person voice) are unchanged
tasks.summary: Replaced pr-revise/SKILL.md Step 8b's hardcoded dev-office.md voice-profile path with a router-based lookup through voice-routing.json's skills/profiles maps, mirroring the mechanism already used by pr-code-review; all other Step 8b instructions are unchanged.

tasks.title: Extend register-skill's registration chain to assign voice-routing.json entries
tasks.description: Add a new step to claude-code-shared/skills/register-skill/SKILL.md's skill-registration path (alongside existing steps 2d/2e for model-tiers.json), that prompts for or defaults a voice profile for the skill being registered and writes the assignment into voice-routing.json's skills map, mirroring how step 2d already writes into model-tiers.json's skills map. Add a corresponding verify step (mirroring 2f) confirming the new skill's entry exists and resolves to a real profile file.
acceptance_criteria: register-skill/SKILL.md documents a new step that writes a skill's voice-routing.json skills-map entry during skill registration
The new step includes a verify check analogous to existing 2f, confirming the skill's entry in voice-routing.json resolves to an existing profile file
Manual check: re-registering pr-code-review or pr-revise following the new step resolves to an entry already matching dev-office (idempotent, no duplicate/broken entries)
tasks.summary: Added step 2d2 to register-skill/SKILL.md's skill-registration path that assigns a skill's voice-routing.json skills-map entry (mirroring 2d for model-tiers.json), with an explicit note that it has no sync-script analog to 2e. Extended step 2f's verify checklist with a fourth check confirming a skill's voice-routing.json entry resolves through profiles to an existing file under resources/voice-profiles/.
